title: [FIRRTL] Temporary names picked over better names
body:
Consider the following:
```
circuit ClockSpec :
  module ClockSpec :
    input clock : Clock
    input gate : UInt<1>
    input in : UInt<8>
    output out : UInt<8>

    wire _internalClock : Clock
    node _gatedClock_T = asUInt(clock)
    node _gatedClock_T_2 = or(_gatedClock_T, gate)
    node gatedClock = asClock(_gatedClock_T_2)
    _internalClock <= gatedClock
    reg r : UInt<8>, gatedClock
    out <= r
    r <= in
```
firtool 1.61.0 gives:
```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.61.0
module ClockSpec(
  input        clock,
               gate,
  input  [7:0] in,
  output [7:0] out
);

  wire       _gatedClock_T_2 = clock | gate;
  reg  [7:0] r;
  always @(posedge _gatedClock_T_2)
    r <= in;
  assign out = r;
endmodule
```
SFC 1.6.0 gives:
```verilog
module ClockSpec(
  input        clock,
  input        gate,
  input  [7:0] in,
  output [7:0] out
);
  wire  gatedClock = clock | gate;
  reg [7:0] r;
  assign out = r;
  always @(posedge gatedClock) begin
    r <= in;
  end
endmodule
```

@seldridge noted that "'gatedClock' disappears during canonicalization in HW Dialect"

Ideally we would pick `gatedClock` over both `_internalClock` and `_gatedClock_T_2` here.
